page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring dedicated physical execution resources without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory-to-core ratio offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage and hardware accelerators represents a tradeoff, making the instance unsuitable for high-IOPS local databases or deep learning tasks. It is instead optimized for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that demand consistent CPU performance and a large memory footprint.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance allocates one thread per physical core to ensure predictable execution. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that performance remains consistent under sustained compute loads, making it an appropriate choice for memory-bound enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a high memory-to-CPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPU resources
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
